name: machine-adc
description: Analog-to-Digital Converter. Read analog voltages on ADC-capable GPIO pins. 12-bit SAR ADC.
differences_from_cpython: Does NOT exist in CPython. MicroPython-specific ESP32 ADC interface.

classes.name: ADC

constructor_params.name: atten
constructor_params.description: Attenuation level. Controls voltage input range.

methods.name: ADC.read_u16
methods.description: Read ADC value scaled to 16-bit range.

methods.name: ADC.read_uv

returns.description: Microvolts. Returns calibrated voltage if efuse calibration data is available.

methods.description: Read calibrated voltage in microvolts. ESP32-specific.

methods.name: ADC.block

returns.description: ADCBlock object for advanced channel configuration.

methods.description: Return ADCBlock for advanced settings. ESP32-specific.

methods.name: ADC.atten
methods.description: Get or set attenuation. value: ADC.ATTN_0DB, ATTN_2_5DB, ATTN_6DB, ATTN_11DB.

methods.name: ADC.width
methods.description: Get/set ADC resolution. bits: WIDTH_9BIT to WIDTH_12BIT.

classes.example:
from machine import Pin, ADC

adc = ADC(Pin(34))
adc.atten(ADC.ATTN_11DB)
val = adc.read()       # 0-4095
uv = adc.read_uv()     # microvolts (if calibrated)
print(f'Raw: {val}, Voltage: {uv/1e6:.3f}V')

esp32_notes: ADC2 cannot be used when Wi-Fi is active (use ADC1 pins: 32-39).
ADC is non-linear; calibrate for accurate voltage readings.
read_uv() requires calibration data stored in efuse (may not be present on all boards).
ADC input impedance is ~100kΩ; use a buffer for high-impedance sources.
